description: Transition from building functional software to engineering resilient, attack-resistant architectures. This roadmap bridges cryptographic foundations, defensive API design, secure identity authentication loops, and the mitigation of production vulnerabilities outlined by industry security baselines.

topics.title: Encryption Basics & Cryptographic Primitives
topics.note: Skip high-level math theories; focus entirely on the application of secure hashing, symmetric/asymmetric encryption, and data-at-rest protection.

subtopics.title: Implement password hashing using Argon2id or bcrypt, configuring appropriate work factors to resist GPU-accelerated brute-force attacks
Master symmetric encryption using AES-256-GCM to securely encrypt sensitive database columns (like API keys or user PI) with authenticated data validation
resources: Cryptographic Right Answers: Symmetric Encryption Patterns

subtopics.title: Implement asymmetric encryption using RSA or ECC (Elliptic Curve Cryptography) to sign data packets and verify payload signatures securely
resources: Practical Cryptography for Developers: Asymmetric Keys Guide

topics.title: Production Authentication & Identity Management
topics.note: Move beyond basic session logins to construct production identity loops, secure token flows, and access control models.

subtopics.title: Architect a stateless JWT authentication layer using short-lived Access Tokens paired with secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict cookies for Refresh Tokens
resources: OWASP JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
Beating JWT Vulnerabilities: Algorithm Confusion and Hijacking

subtopics.title: Build a secure OAuth2 / OIDC integration flow (e.g., Sign in with GitHub) using Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) to eliminate interception vulnerabilities
resources: OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice (RFC 8252 + Updates)

subtopics.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) middleware to strictly enforce multi-tenant API authorization boundaries
resources: Designing Fine-Grained Authorization Middleware in Python Backends

topics.title: API Hardening & Defensive Gateways
topics.note: Secure your endpoints from automated scrapers, denial-of-service, and malicious request parameters.

subtopics.title: Implement sliding-window token bucket Rate Limiting middleware at the gateway layer utilizing Redis for tracking IP-based and User-ID-based requests
resources: Defensive API Engineering: Rate Limiting Architectures with Redis

subtopics.title: Configure comprehensive Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) rules and strict Content Security Policies (CSP) to systematically drop unauthorized script execution requests
resources: MDN Web Docs: Securing Headers and Web Applications

subtopics.title: Incorporate Input Validation and Strict Schema Enforcement pipelines (using tools like Pydantic or Zod) to eliminate malformed JSON attacks and parameter pollution
resources: Data Validation as a Security Control Boundary

topics.title: OWASP Threat Mitigation (Top 10 Web + API)
topics.note: Gain practical engineering answers to the absolute most common critical vulnerabilities targeting internet-exposed web vectors.

subtopics.title: Defend against Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA/IDOR) by replacing guessable sequential IDs with cryptographically secure UUIDv4 indices matched against explicit context permissions
resources: OWASP API Security Top 10: API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization

subtopics.title: Neutralize SQL Injection (SQLi) and Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerabilities by strictly using parameterization layers and domain allowlists for downstream requests
resources: Preventing Server-Side Request Forgery in AI Inference Backends

subtopics.title: Secure against Mass Assignment and Broken Function Level Authorization by structuring explicit white-listed Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) for incoming payloads
resources: Defensive Web Patterns: Securing API Routes from Mass Assignment

topics.title: Security Hardening Capstone
topics.note: Execute a full-stack security review and infrastructure penetration simulation on your own codebase ecosystem.

subtopics.title: Refactor your complete full-stack application backend to handle securely encrypted runtime parameters: introduce AES-256 field-level model encryption for API keys and implement rotating JWTs with token revocation lists via Redis

Run a comprehensive vulnerability automation assessment: integrate SonarQube, Snyk, and OWASP ZAP into your local container cluster, analyze the threat vectors, and execute patches for every reported flag
resources: Automating Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) with OWASP ZAP
